page: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, offering 5669 GB of local disk capacity with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the E96ds_v7 demonstrates strong performance. The Intel Xeon processor delivers top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng are rated in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached operations and large block uncached operations scoring in the top 10%. The system also excels in data processing, achieving top-tier ratings in gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which reach 159.97 million operations per second. For AI workloads, the server supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, enabling top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage, the E96ds_v7 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, data warehousing, and heavy web serving. The inclusion of a 5669 GB local disk provides a cost-efficient alternative to external block storage for high-throughput, temporary data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores), 768.0 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and data decompression. Benchmark results highlight its strength in Redis SET operations, achieving 159.97 million operations per second, and its efficiency in processing medium-sized language model prompts using Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions. Lacking a GPU, it is designed for memory-intensive, non-graphical workloads. The E96ds_v7 is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, high-throughput web serving, and enterprise-grade data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is an Edsv7 server with 96 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized platform with ninety-six dedicated Intel vCPUs, extensive local storage, and top-tier database processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per core.
5669 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis performance of 159.97 million operations per second.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated CPU-based AI prompt processing.
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data warehousing.
